name: Database Backup and Point-in-Time Recovery with RDS
description: Create manual RDS snapshots, configure automated backups, and perform point-in-time recovery to restore databases to any second within the retention window.
prerequisites: AWS CLI v2 installed and configured
IAM permissions for RDS snapshot operations

steps.title: Create a manual snapshot
steps.description: Take an immediate snapshot of the RDS instance to capture the current state of the database before making changes.
steps.tip: Always create a manual snapshot before schema migrations or major updates. Manual snapshots persist until you explicitly delete them, unlike automated backups.

steps.title: Wait for the snapshot to complete
steps.description: Monitor the snapshot creation progress and wait until it reaches the available state.
steps.command: aws rds wait db-snapshot-available --db-snapshot-identifier my-production-db-pre-migration-$(date +%Y%m%d) && echo 'Snapshot ready'
steps.warning: Snapshot creation time depends on database size and can take from minutes to hours for large databases. The database remains available during snapshot creation but may experience brief I/O suspension.

steps.title: Check the restore window for point-in-time recovery
steps.description: Determine the earliest and latest times you can restore to using the automated backup retention window.
steps.tip: LatestRestorableTime is typically within the last 5 minutes. The restore window extends back by the number of days in BackupRetentionPeriod.

steps.title: Restore to a point in time
steps.description: Create a new RDS instance restored to a specific point in time. This creates a completely new database instance, leaving the original untouched.
steps.command: aws rds restore-db-instance-to-point-in-time --source-db-instance-identifier my-production-db --target-db-instance-identifier my-production-db-restored --restore-time 2026-03-11T08:30:00Z --db-instance-class db.t3.medium --vpc-security-group-ids sg-0example
steps.warning: Point-in-time restore creates a new instance with a new endpoint. You must update your application's connection string to point to the restored instance.

steps.title: Monitor restore progress
steps.description: Wait for the restored instance to become available and check its status.
steps.command: aws rds wait db-instance-available --db-instance-identifier my-production-db-restored && aws rds describe-db-instances --db-instance-identifier my-production-db-restored --query 'DBInstances[0].{Status:DBInstanceStatus,Endpoint:Endpoint.Address,Port:Endpoint.Port}' --output table

steps.title: Clean up old snapshots
steps.description: List and delete snapshots older than your retention policy to manage storage costs.
steps.command: aws rds describe-db-snapshots --db-instance-identifier my-production-db --snapshot-type manual --query 'DBSnapshots | sort_by(@, &SnapshotCreateTime) | [*].{Snapshot:DBSnapshotIdentifier,Created:SnapshotCreateTime,Size:AllocatedStorage}' --output table
steps.tip: Automated snapshots are free up to your allocated storage size. Manual snapshots incur standard EBS snapshot storage charges at approximately $0.095/GB-month.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# RDS Snapshot and Point-in-Time Recovery
# Usage: ./rds-backup.sh <action> <db-identifier> [restore-time]
# Actions: snapshot, restore, list, cleanup

set -euo pipefail

ACTION="${1:?Usage: $0 <snapshot|restore|list|cleanup> <db-identifier> [restore-time]}"
DB_ID="${2:?Provide RDS instance identifier}"

case "$ACTION" in
  snapshot)
    SNAPSHOT_ID="$DB_ID-manual-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
    echo "Creating snapshot: $SNAPSHOT_ID"
    aws rds create-db-snapshot \
      --db-instance-identifier "$DB_ID" \
      --db-snapshot-identifier "$SNAPSHOT_ID"

    echo "Waiting for snapshot to complete..."
    aws rds wait db-snapshot-available --db-snapshot-identifier "$SNAPSHOT_ID"
    echo "Snapshot complete: $SNAPSHOT_ID"
    ;;

  restore)
    RESTORE_TIME="${3:?Provide restore time in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2026-03-11T08:30:00Z)}"
    TARGET_ID="$DB_ID-restored-$(date +%s)"
    echo "Restoring $DB_ID to $RESTORE_TIME as $TARGET_ID ..."

    aws rds restore-db-instance-to-point-in-time \
      --source-db-instance-identifier "$DB_ID" \
      --target-db-instance-identifier "$TARGET_ID" \
      --restore-time "$RESTORE_TIME"

    echo "Waiting for restored instance..."
    aws rds wait db-instance-available --db-instance-identifier "$TARGET_ID"

    ENDPOINT=$(aws rds describe-db-instances \
      --db-instance-identifier "$TARGET_ID" \
      --query 'DBInstances[0].Endpoint.Address' --output text)
    echo "Restored instance available at: $ENDPOINT"
    ;;

  list)
    echo "=== Automated Backups ==="
    aws rds describe-db-instances --db-instance-identifier "$DB_ID" \
      --query 'DBInstances[0].{LatestRestore:LatestRestorableTime,RetentionDays:BackupRetentionPeriod}' \
      --output table

    echo ""
    echo "=== Manual Snapshots ==="
    aws rds describe-db-snapshots \
      --db-instance-identifier "$DB_ID" \
      --snapshot-type manual \
      --query 'DBSnapshots | sort_by(@, &SnapshotCreateTime) | reverse(@) | [*].{Snapshot:DBSnapshotIdentifier,Created:SnapshotCreateTime,SizeGB:AllocatedStorage,Status:Status}' \
      --output table
    ;;

  cleanup)
    DAYS="${3:-30}"
    CUTOFF=$(date -d "$DAYS days ago" +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)
    echo "Finding manual snapshots older than $DAYS days (before $CUTOFF)..."

    aws rds describe-db-snapshots \
      --db-instance-identifier "$DB_ID" \
      --snapshot-type manual \
      --query "DBSnapshots[?SnapshotCreateTime<'$CUTOFF'].DBSnapshotIdentifier" \
      --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | while read -r SNAP; do
        echo "Deleting: $SNAP"
        aws rds delete-db-snapshot --db-snapshot-identifier "$SNAP"
      done
    echo "Cleanup complete."
    ;;

  *)
    echo "Unknown action: $ACTION (use snapshot, restore, list, or cleanup)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

faq.question: Does creating a snapshot cause downtime?
faq.answer: No. RDS snapshots are taken from the underlying EBS volumes without stopping the database. For single-AZ instances there may be a brief I/O pause (typically a few seconds). Multi-AZ instances take the snapshot from the standby, so there is no impact on the primary.

faq.question: Can I restore a snapshot to a different instance class or engine version?
faq.answer: You can restore to a different instance class (size). For engine version upgrades, restore first and then modify the instance to upgrade. You cannot downgrade engine versions.

faq.question: How long does point-in-time recovery take?
faq.answer: Recovery time depends on database size and the amount of transaction log replay needed. A small database may take 10-15 minutes while a large database with heavy write activity can take an hour or more. The restored instance is a completely new RDS instance with a new endpoint.
